rulings.Case Title: JASON GILL VS. OFFICE OF SHORT TERM RENTALS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 9, 2021 line 1. RESPONDENT OFFICE OF SHORT TERM RENTALS, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is continued to September 23, 2021 per stipulation and order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN DORGAN ELECTRICAL, INC., A CORPORATION VS. EDMUND CHUTE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Edmund Chute For Judgment On The Pleadings As To The First, Third, And Fourth Causes Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 9, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT EDMUND CHUTE Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Edmund Chute For Judgment On The Pleadings As To The First, Third, And Fourth Causes Of Action is DENIED. No determination can be made based on the allegations in the complaint and judicially noticeable documents to establish the first element of judicial estoppel, i.e. that the same party has taken two positions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: REZA R. DIBADJ VS. AMERICAN CONTRACTORS INDEMNITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 9, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT AMERICAN CONTRACTORS INDEMNITY COMPANY DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is continued to October 5, 2021. By no later than September 25, 2021 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief limited to citation to cases, with no further argument, factually similar to the case at bar brought by a similarly situated plaintiff under (1) B&P 7071.5(a) and (2) B&P 7071.5(c); e.g. cases in which a neighbor was found a third-party beneficiary; cases in which willful and deliberate violation was alleged, etc. If Plaintiff is unable to locate such cases, Plaintiff shall state "none." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: 452 CLEMENTINA, LLC VS. KENDALL BROWN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 9, 2021 line 9. DEFENDANT KENDALL BROWN DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Defendant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that an owner/landlord must be identified in the operative notice. Assuming, without so deciding that SFRO 37.9(n) is applicable, Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts (Notice is incorporated) to establish that the termination is based on violence/threats of violence/health and safety. Furthermore, assuming arguendo, that Defendant's interpretation of the lease provision as limiting the definition of nuisance is correct (generally on a demurrer Plaintiff's reasonable interpretation of the contract is accepted), the Notice alleges at least three separate incidents: (1) on May 16, 2021 baseball bat used to beat the back door of a vacant unit; (2) on May 30, 2021 personal possessions of another tenant taken; (3) on May 30, 2021 personal possession of another tenant thrown over the fence; (4) thereafter, personal possession doused with lighter fluid; (5) thereafter, neighbor's construction materials and carboard boxes doused with lighter fluid; (6) then, lit wooden matches thrown over the fence seeking to cause a fire. Plaintiff alleges that this tenancy is subject to SFRO and that this eviction is not based on non-payment of rent. Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that sections 6 and 10(a)-(c) of the supplemental portion on UD-101 must be filled out. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CONARD HOUSE INC. VS. EREZ BARAK KOMINEK ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 9, 2021 line 10. DEFENDANT EREZ KOMINEK DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. Demurrer properly raised a potential issue of inconsistency between allegations in the Complaint and Supplemental Complaint/Cover Sheet. Complaint identifies only one named defendant. UD-101 identifies that no defendants fall under the definition of CC 1940(b) in section 3(b)(1) but omits the name of the sole named defendant in section 3(b)(2). Since meet and confer requirements on a demurrer are not applicable to unlawful detainer action, Plaintiff was alerted to its incomplete form via a demurrer and corrected the omission by re-filing the form properly filled out. Re-filing of form UD-101 resolved the perceived inconsistency. Moving party cites no authority for the proposition that UD-101 is not subject to CCP 473 authorizing one amendment without leave of court. The operative pleadings are now comprised of the August 9, 2021 Complaint and August 18, 2021 UD-101. Defendant shall have 5 days to respond to the operative pleading. The Court notes that Plaintiff altered the mandatory form without so indicating on the form itself (the word amended appears in the same font as the rest as the title). Plaintiff shall refrain from altering mandatory form in the future, especially in such fashion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
